Predict the count of the queried letter. Fresh data every time.
generator:
import random
from collections import Counter
def make():
    data = [random.choice("abc") for _ in range(random.randint(6, 10))]
    q = random.choice("abc")
    snippet = "from collections import Counter\nc = Counter(" + repr(data) + ")\nc[" + repr(q) + "]"
    return {"snippet": snippet, "expected": repr(Counter(data)[q])}

prompt: Implement `biggest(a)` returning the largest element — by hand, no `max()`. Fresh data every time.
generator:
import random
def make():
    a = [random.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(random.randint(4, 7))]
    starter = "def biggest(a):
    # your code here
    pass"
    solution = "def biggest(a):
    m = a[0]
    for i in range(1, len(a)):
        if a[i] > m:
            m = a[i]
    return m"
    tests = "assert biggest(" + repr(a) + ") == " + repr(max(a))
    return {"starter": starter, "solution": solution, "tests": tests, "banned": ["max("]}
